test.name: Recommend completing in-progress plan before adding new questions
test.description: The mid-research-flynn scenario has q_001 (parentage) in_progress with pli_006 (probate search) still in_progress. The skill should recognize that there's pending in-flight work and recommend completing that before formulating a new question. Tests the 'don't churn — finish what's open' aspect of question-selection.

additional_criteria: Should recognize that pli_006 (probate search for Thomas Flynn 1870-1890) is still in_progress and that the in-flight plan should be completed before introducing new questions
Should NOT create a new `q_` question at this point — adding new questions while existing ones have pending plan items churns the research direction without resolving anything

If the skill does recommend a follow-up question, it should be one that depends on the probate result (e.g., a sibling-identification question that follows from a will naming children) and explicitly mark `depends_on: ["q_001"]`
Should reference the specific plan item (`pli_006`) by ID — not just 'the probate search' — so the recommendation is anchored in research.json state
